content: The sequential layered structure of human vocabulary acquisition for the word 'reflexion' (from basic memorization to contextual reflective practice) can be framed as a tiny endogenous analog of the LLM iterative improvement pipeline that combines Reflexion's dynamic memory storage and Self-Refine's iterative feedback refinement. This framework reveals that even implicit human lexical learning follows the same core pattern of improvement that cutting-edge AI agents are only now being designed to replicate.
